pattern: The same additive grid count as the obstacle-free version, except any blocked cell contributes zero paths, which naturally zeroes out everything downstream of it.
hints: A cell's path count is the sum of the ways into the cell above and the cell to its left - unless the cell itself is blocked.
An obstacle has zero paths through it, and so does the start cell if it is itself blocked.
Initialize a table, set blocked cells to 0, seed the entrance to 1 (if open), and fill row by row with dp[i][j] = dp[i-1][j] + dp[i][j-1] whenever grid[i][j] is open.

edge_inputs.description: obstacle in the center of a three by three grid
blocked start cell yields zero paths
blocked destination cell yields zero paths
an obstacle wall fully severs the only corridor
single open cell has one path

stress.description: random grids with sparse obstacles, kept small enough that counts are exact integers
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    m = rng.randint(1, 30)
    n = rng.randint(1, 30)
    grid = [[1 if rng.random() < 0.2 else 0 for _ in range(n)] for _ in range(m)]
    grid[0][0] = 0
    grid[m - 1][n - 1] = 0
    return [grid]
stress.note: entrance and exit forced open; integer DP keeps results exact in both languages
